source: A Quick Review of E-PCM for Engineering Entrance - Physics
subject: Physics

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A ball of mass 1 kg falls from a height of 5 m above the free surface of water. The relative density of the solid ball is 5/3. The ball travels a distance of 2 m under water and becomes stationary. The work done by the resistive forces of water is:

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A balloon has volume of 1000 m³. It is filled with hydrogen (ρ = 0.09 g/litre). If the density of air is 1.29 g/litre, it can lift a total weight of:

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: The hydraulic press shown in the figure is used to raise the mass M through a height of 5.0 mm by performing 500 J of work at the small piston. The diameter of the large piston is 10 cm while that of the smaller one is 2 cm. The mass M is:
questions.explanation: From conservation of volume: a×l×s = A×5mm → y = 125 mm. W = Fy → F = 500/0.125 = 4000 N. M/m = (A/a) = (10/2)² = 25. M = 400×25 = 10⁴ kg

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A container has a small hole at its bottom. Area of cross-section of the hole is A₁ and that of the container is A₂. Liquid is poured in the container at a constant rate Q m³/s. The maximum level of liquid in the container will be:
questions.explanation: At maximum height, dh/dt = 0, so Q = A₁√(2gh). h = Q²/(2gA₁²)

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: For what value of x the range on the horizontal ground from both the holes will be same (holes at depth x and h from top in a container of height h):
questions.explanation: For equal range from two holes at depths x and h, we need equal range which requires symmetric placement. Given the constraints, they can't be equal.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: Two substances of densities ρ₁ and ρ₂ are mixed in equal volume and the relative density of mixture is 4. When they are mixed in equal masses, the relative density of the mixture is 3. The values of ρ₁ and ρ₂ are:
questions.explanation: Equal volumes: (ρ₁+ρ₂)/2 = 4 → ρ₁+ρ₂ = 8. Equal masses: 2ρ₁ρ₂/(ρ₁+ρ₂) = 3 → ρ₁ρ₂ = 12. Solving: ρ₁ = 6, ρ₂ = 2

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A cubical block of wood of specific gravity 0.5 and a chunk of concrete of specific gravity 2.5 are fastened together. The ratio of the mass of wood to the mass of concrete which makes the combination to float with its entire volume submerged under water is:
questions.explanation: For neutral buoyancy: m_w + m_c = (m_w/0.5 + m_c/2.5)×1. Solving gives m_w/m_c = 1/3

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A U-tube containing a liquid is accelerated horizontally with a constant acceleration a. If the separation between the two vertical limbs is l, then the difference in the heights of the liquid in the two arms is:
questions.explanation: tan θ = a/g = Δh/l → Δh = la/g

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A cube is floating in liquid. Now temperature is increased considering the expansion of cube only. Then:
questions.optionA: both x and y will increase
questions.optionB: x will decrease while y will remain constant
questions.optionC: both x and y will decrease
questions.optionD: x will decrease while y will increase

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A solid of density D is floating in a liquid of density d. If v is the volume of solid submerged in the liquid and V is the total volume of the solid, then v/V is equal to:

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A cubical block of side 10 cm floats at the interface of an oil and water. The pressure above that of atmosphere at the lower face of the block is:

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: Pressure inside two soap bubbles are 1.01 and 1.02 atmosphere. Ratio of their volume is:
questions.explanation: Excess pressure P ∝ 1/r. P₁/P₂ = 0.01/0.02 = 1/2 → r₁/r₂ = 2. V ∝ r³ → V₁/V₂ = 8:1. But given answer is d (1:8), so ratio asked may be smaller to bigger.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: Two equal drops of water are falling through air with a steady velocity v. If the drops coalesce, the new velocity will be:

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: Water flows along a horizontal pipe of which the cross-section is not constant. The pressure is 1 cm of Hg where the velocity is 35 cm/s. At a point where the velocity is 65 cm/s, the pressure will be:
questions.explanation: P₁ + ½ρv₁² = P₂ + ½ρv₂². P₂ = 1 cm Hg + ½×1000×(0.35² - 0.65²)/(13600×9.8) = 0.89 cm of Hg

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: The pressure of water in a pipe when tap is closed is 5.5 × 10⁵ N/m². When tap gets open, pressure reduces to 5 × 10⁵ N/m². The velocity with which water comes out on opening the tap is:

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: When a large bubble rises from the bottom of a lake to its surface, its radius doubles. The atmospheric pressure is equal to that of a column of liquid A of height 70 cm. The depth of the lake is (sp. gravity of the liquid = 15.0):
questions.explanation: PV = const. P_atm × (4/3)π(2r)³ = (P_atm + hρ_wg) × (4/3)πr³. 7P_atm = hρ_wg. h = 7×0.7×15 = 73.5 m

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A piece of wax weighs x g in air. A piece of metal is found to weigh y g in water. It is tied to the wax and both together weigh z g in water. Then the specific gravity of wax is (z < y):
questions.explanation: Specific gravity of wax = x/(x-(z-y)) = x/(x-z+y)

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A large block of ice 5 m thick has a vertical hole drilled through it and is floating in the middle of a lake. The minimum length of the rope required to scoop up a bucket of water through the hole is (density of ice = 0.9 g/cc):
questions.explanation: Ice of 5 m × ρ_ice = l × ρ_water → l = 5 × 0.9 = 4.5 m submerged. Length outside water = 5 - 4.5 = 0.5 m

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: Water is flowing through two horizontal pipes of different diameters which are connected together. The diameters of the two pipes are 3 cm and 6 cm respectively. If the speed of water in the narrower tube is 4 m/s. Then the speed of water in the wider tube is:
questions.explanation: A₁V₁ = A₂V₂. V₂ = (r₁/r₂)² × V₁ = (1.5/3)² × 4 = (1/4) × 4 = 1 m/s

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: Eight drops of water each of radius 2 mm are falling through air at a terminal velocity of 8 cm/sec. If they coalesce to form a single drop, the terminal velocity of the combined drop is:

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A drum of radius R full of liquid of density d is rotated about an axis touching its surface at ω rad/sec. The increase in pressure at the centre of the drum will be:

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A rain drop of radius 0.3 mm has a terminal velocity of 1 m/s in air. The viscosity of air is 18 × 10⁻⁵ poise. Then the viscous force on the drop will be:

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A hole is made at the bottom of the tank filled with water (density 1000 kg/m³). If the total pressure at the bottom of the tank is 3 atmosphere (1 atmosphere = 10⁵ N/m²) then the velocity of efflux is:

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: Bernoulli's equation is a consequence of conservation of:
questions.explanation: Bernoulli's theorem is based on conservation of energy.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A tank is filled to a height H. The range of water coming out of a hole which is at a depth H/4 from the surface of water level is:

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: There is a hole of area A at the bottom of a cylindrical vessel. Water is filled up to a height h and water flows out in 't' second. If water is filled to a height 4h, it will flow out in time:
questions.explanation: Time to empty ∝ √h. t₂/t₁ = √(4h/h) = 2. t₂ = 2t

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: An ice-cube of density 900 kg/m³ is floating in water of density 1000 kg/m³. The percentage of volume of ice-cube outside the water is:
questions.explanation: % outside = (1 - ρ_ice/ρ_water) × 100 = (1 - 900/1000) × 100 = 10%. Closest answer is 11%.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A raft of wood of mass 120 kg floats in water. The weight that can be put on the raft to make it just sink should be (ρ_wood = 600 kg/m³):
questions.explanation: V = 120/600 = 0.2 m³. Max buoyancy = 0.2 × 1000 = 200 kg. Extra weight = 200 - 120 = 80 kg
